title: Bridge Essay: Beyond the Brain: Exploring the multi-organ axes in Parkinson's disease pathogen

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Breath and soul — pranayama, breath-brain coupling, respiratory-brain interaction, cardiorespiratory entrainment, vagal pathways of consciousness.

**Beyond the Brain: Exploring the multi-organ axes in Parkinson'sdisease pathogenesis.** — J Adv Res. PMID: 40383292. DOI: 10.1016/j.jare.2025.05.034. Parkinson's Disease (PD), acomplex neurodegenerative disorder, is increasingly recognized as asystemic condition involving multi-organ interactions. Emerging evidence highlights roles of organ-brain axes (lung-, liver-, heart-, muscle-, bone-, and gut-brain) in PD pathogenesis. These axes communicate via neural, circulatory, endocrine, and inflammatory pathways, collectively driving neurodegeneration.
